commits.subject: fix(theme): restore custom CSS silently dropped by broken comment, finish semantic token migration
commits.body:
The previous commit on this branch (c7a3469) claimed a 16% CSS bundle
reduction (147 KB -> 123 KB). That reduction turned out to be a build
artifact of a broken CSS comment, not a genuine savings.

Root cause:

In the rewritten :root block at the top of dashboard/styles.css, the
migration-rationale comment contained the literal text:

    - Keep legacy --bg-*/--text-*/--border-* aliases: Rejected — ...

The `*/` sequence inside `--bg-*/--text-*` terminates the CSS comment
early (at `--bg-*`), and the parser sees everything after it as bad CSS.
Tailwind v4 / esbuild's minifier silently drops everything following the
parse error, which meant ~27 KB of custom class definitions were never
emitted to the built bundle:

  - All .heatmap-* classes (intensity levels 0-4, cell, label, header,
    grid, tooltip-inner)
  - All .filter-multi-select* classes (dropdown, option, trigger)
  - All .settings-pane* classes (overlay, header, content, close, title)
  - All .detail-pane* classes (overlay, header, close, content, empty,
    loading, subtitle, title)
  - .root-error-message, .root-error-detail, .root-error-hint,
    .error-boundary-card
  - Several others

The build passed with no top-level errors — the "Unexpected token Delim
('*')" was buried as Issue #1 inside the "Found 62 warnings while
optimizing generated CSS" summary, alongside DaisyUI's unrelated
@property / @keyframes warnings. The dashboard would have shipped with
heatmaps, detail panes, settings panes, and filter multi-select all
rendering without their custom CSS, falling back to browser defaults.

Fix:

1. Rephrased the comment in dashboard/styles.css to avoid the `*/`
   sequence. "Keep legacy --bg-*/--text-*/--border-* aliases" became
   "Keep legacy --bg, --text, --border aliases" with a trailing note
   warning future sessions never to write asterisk-slash inside CSS
   comments.

2. Added a CLAUDE.md Frontend checklist item prohibiting asterisk-slash
   sequences inside CSS comments and explaining why.

3. Added a detailed AI_MISTAKES.md entry with the smoke-test bash snippet
   that would have caught this.

Verification:

After the fix, the built CSS is 150.6 KB (vs the misleading 123 KB from
the broken build, and vs the pre-migration 147.16 KB). The +3.5 KB delta
is DaisyUI's theme selector blocks + semantic component classes being
added, partially offset by deleted custom variables. Verified all
critical custom class families are present via curl against the preview
server:

  OK: .heatmap-0 .heatmap-1 .heatmap-2 .heatmap-3 .heatmap-4
  OK: .heatmap-cell .heatmap-tooltip-inner

commits.subject: refactor(theme): complete DaisyUI v5 migration — all six reference phases
commits.body:
Follow-up to the initial "infrastructure + partial" commit on this branch.
User requested no shortcuts, so this completes Phases 1-6 of the reference
doc (docs/implementations/THEME_DARK_MODE.md) in a single pass: full CSS
variable removal, component migration across 21 files, and cleanup.

Phase 1 — Audit:
  202 var(--bg-*/--text-*/--border-*/--color-*/--chart-grid/--glow-*/--shadow*)
  references in styles.css, 166 text-themed-*/bg-themed-*/border-themed
  references across 21 JSX files, 39 dark: Tailwind pairs across 10 JSX
  files, and ~15 hardcoded rgba(45, 104, 255, ...) brand-blue values.

Phase 2 — Custom variable removal (dashboard/styles.css):
  Rewrote :root to hold ONLY non-theme tokens (spacing, radius, z-index,
  fonts). Deleted the entire html.dark override block — DaisyUI's
  [data-theme] selectors handle switching now. Bulk-replaced via
  replace_all:
    var(--bg-primary/secondary/tertiary/hover) →
      var(--color-base-100/200/300/300)
    var(--text-primary) → var(--color-base-content)
    var(--text-secondary/tertiary/muted) →
      color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-base-content) 80%/60%/40%, transparent)
    var(--border-color/light) → var(--color-base-300/200)
    var(--glow-primary) →
      0 0 20px color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-primary) 25%, transparent)
    var(--chart-grid) →
      color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-base-content) 10%, transparent)
    var(--color-primary-alpha) →
      color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-primary) 10%, transparent)

  Replaced hardcoded brand-blue rgba(45, 104, 255, X) values with color-mix
  against var(--color-primary) — focus rings, btn-primary hover, drop-zone
  states, tab-btn-active text-shadow, body::before techy grid background.
  The brand accent now follows the active theme's primary color.

  Deleted 3 legacy CSS blocks: Tailwind gray overrides (.bg-gray-50/100,
  .text-gray-400/500/600/700/900, .border-gray-100/200, etc.), semantic
  card backgrounds (.bg-amber-50, .bg-indigo-50, ..., .bg-red-50), and
  semantic text colors (.text-green-600, .text-red-600, .text-amber-600,
  .text-blue-600, .border-blue-500).

  Deleted 8 utility class definitions: .text-themed-primary/secondary/
  tertiary/muted, .bg-themed-primary/secondary/tertiary, .border-themed.

  Swapped color: white / #1a1a1a / #fff hardcoded foregrounds to DaisyUI
  *-content tokens in .toast-success/error/warning, .btn-primary,
  .quick-guide-btn-primary, .filter-preset-btn:hover/.active,
  .filter-mode-toggle button.active, .project-link-primary, .filter-badge.

  .install-modal-note amber rgba literals → color-mix against warning.
  .drop-zone-heading color: #e5e7eb → var(--color-base-content).

  Print styles @media print intentionally keep hardcoded white/black/#e5e7eb
  per the reference doc's Print Override section.

Phase 3 — Component migration (21 JSX files, all 39 dark: pairs):
  Zero dark: class names remain in JSX (only in migration comments).

  166 text-themed-*/bg-themed-*/border-themed references migrated to
  DaisyUI Tailwind utilities:
    text-themed-primary → text-base-content
    text-themed-secondary/tertiary/muted →
      text-base-content/80, /60, /40
    bg-themed-primary/secondary/tertiary → bg-base-100/200/300
    border-themed → border-base-300

  Summary.jsx Activity Snapshot cards: 4 data viz pastel bg/dark pairs →
    bg-warning/15 + text-warning (after-hours),
    bg-info/15 + text-info (weekend),
    bg-accent/15 + text-accent (holiday),
    bg-secondary/15 + text-secondary (complex).
  Timeline.jsx complexity badges: purple/blue/gray tier colors →
    bg-secondary/20 (high) / bg-info/20 (medium) / bg-base-300 (low).
  Health.jsx security panels: all bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-900/20 + border-
    red-200/800 + text-red-600/400 → bg-error/10 + border-error/40
    + text-error.
  HealthBars, HealthAnomalies, Contributors, Tags, Timing, Progress,
    Discover: progress-bar rails → bg-base-300, hover states →
    hover:bg-base-200 / hover:bg-base-300.

  App.jsx embed override now sets --color-base-100 (DaisyUI token) instead
  of the removed --bg-primary alias, and toggles data-theme alongside .dark.

  Tags.jsx removed its per-component useChartTextColor hook and explicit
  color: CHART_TEXT_COLOR overrides — now relies on ChartJS.defaults.color
  (kept fresh by AppContext's darkMode effect) as the single source of
  truth. Chart rebuilds on theme toggle via existing state.darkMode useMemo
  dep.

Chart.js theme sync:
  AppContext.jsx darkMode effect no longer reads removed --text-secondary /
  --chart-grid. It reads DaisyUI's --color-base-content oklch token
  directly and passes color-mix(in oklab, X 80%/10%, transparent) to
  ChartJS.defaults.color / borderColor. Canvas parses color-mix() since
  Chrome 111 / Firefox 113 / Safari 16.2 — same baseline as DaisyUI.

Phase 5 — Verification:
  TESTING_GUIDE.md Theme section rewritten with full 10-point checklist:
  system preference, dual-layer attributes, flash prevention, cross-tab
  sync, PWA status bar, charts, surface auto-switching, semantic tokens,
  print override. Build passes and Vite preview serves correctly — DaisyUI
  [data-theme="lofi"] and [data-theme="black"] selectors present in the
  served CSS (verified via curl).

Phase 6 — Cleanup:
  Zero custom --bg-*/--text-*/--border-*/--color-primary-alpha/--chart-grid/
  --glow-*/--shadow* variables remain anywhere in the codebase (verified
  via comprehensive Grep — only remaining matches are migration comments).
  Updated CLAUDE.md Frontend standards to enforce DaisyUI semantic tokens
  and prohibit re-introducing the deleted variables.

Build: passes. CSS bundle 147.16 KB → 123.27 KB (−23.9 KB, −16%) from
deleted variables, gray overrides, and utility classes.

Docs updated: CLAUDE.md, docs/SESSION_NOTES.md, docs/HISTORY.md,
docs/TODO.md, docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md, docs/USER_GUIDE.md.

Remaining (optional) future work: replace raw Tailwind / custom-class
elements with DaisyUI component classes (btn, card, input, badge) —
tracked in docs/TODO.md. Not required for the migration to be complete.

commits.subject: feat(theme): migrate to DaisyUI with dual-layer theming and meta theme-color
commits.body:
Partial migration to DaisyUI per docs/implementations/THEME_DARK_MODE.md.
DaisyUI semantic tokens now coexist with the existing custom CSS variable
system — incremental replacement to stay low-risk rather than a big-bang
rewrite of the 2000+ line styles.css.

Infrastructure:

1. Installed daisyui@5 (5.5.19) as devDependency. Registered two themes
   via @plugin "daisyui" { themes: lofi --default, black --prefersdark }
   in styles.css. --default / --prefersdark set the initial theme before
   JavaScript runs. Added @layer base { html { color-scheme: light } html.dark
   { color-scheme: dark } } so native form inputs and scrollbars match.

2. Dual-layer theming wired up end-to-end: <html> gains data-theme="black"
   alongside class="dark" default. Flash prevention inline script in
   index.html now sets BOTH .dark class AND data-theme attribute before
   first paint, and also overwrites <meta name="theme-color"> content so
   the PWA status bar matches the active theme from the first frame.

3. Added two <meta name="theme-color"> tags with (prefers-color-scheme: ...)
   media queries. Defaults #ffffff (lofi base-100) and #000000 (black
   base-100). AppContext.darkMode effect now calls setAttribute('data-theme',
   ...) and overwrites both meta tags on every toggle via new module
   constants LIGHT_THEME/DARK_THEME/LIGHT_META_COLOR/DARK_META_COLOR that
   must stay in sync with the inline script.

4. Cross-tab sync already existed (AppContext storage event listener) —
   verified it still applies .dark, data-theme, and meta theme-color
   correctly via the shared darkMode effect.

Component migration (~30 of 39 dark: pairs collapsed):

5. HealthBars, HealthAnomalies, Contributors, Tags, Timing, Progress,
   Discover, Timeline, Health: neutral gray progress-bar rails and hover
   states → bg-base-300 / hover:bg-base-200 / hover:bg-base-300. Health
   security indicators → bg-error/10, border-error/40, text-error
   (DaisyUI error semantic token is the right fit for security status).

6. NOT migrated (data viz category colors per reference doc): Summary.jsx
   stat cards (amber/indigo/pink/purple for distinct metrics) and
   Timeline.jsx complexity badges (purple=high, blue=medium). These are
   data visualization colors with fixed meaning, not theme tokens.

Remaining work tracked in docs/TODO.md — full removal of 202 var(--color-*)
references from styles.css and component class migration to DaisyUI btn /
card / input / badge classes are still pending and can happen incrementally.

Build passes (./node_modules/.bin/vite build). CSS bundle 147.16 KB →
146.06 KB from collapsed dark: pairs.

commits.subject: refactor(debug): deduplicate helpers, fix strict mode double-mount race
commits.body:
1. Extract formatDebugTime and safeStringify from DebugPill into
   debugLog.js as shared exports. Eliminates duplicate timestamp
   formatting logic (DebugPill formatTime vs inline in report
   generation) and duplicate safe-stringify logic (DebugPill
   safeStringify function vs inline try/catch in report).

2. Fix React strict mode double-mount race: in dev mode, effects
   run twice (mount → cleanup → mount). Without a reset, the second
   subscription replay appends to the first mount's state, doubling
   every entry. Adding setEntries([]) before subscribing ensures
   each mount builds cleanly from empty.

commits.subject: refactor(debug): use debugAdd for pre-React error bridge instead of manual entry construction
commits.body:
The bridge code was bypassing debugAdd() — directly constructing entry
objects, pushing to the internal entries array, and incrementing nextId
manually. If debugAdd changes (new fields, validation, side effects),
the bridge would be out of sync.

Fix: add optional timestamp parameter to debugAdd (defaults to
Date.now()), then call debugAdd from the bridge with the original
error timestamp. All entry construction now flows through one function.

commits.subject: fix(debug): preserve pre-React error timestamps, stop inline pill DOM work
commits.body:
Two fixes to the inline pill / debugLog bridge:

1. Inline pill now stores Date.now() (numeric epoch) instead of
   toLocaleTimeString() (formatted string). Matches the spec's inline
   pill code. The bridge in debugLog.js creates entries with the
   original error timestamp instead of module-load time, so pre-React
   errors show accurate times in the React DebugPill's Log tab.
   Added fmtTime() helper for the inline pill's own display and copy.

2. Inline pill render() now bails early when window.__debugReactMounted
   is true. The error listeners still fire (can't remove from IIFE
   closure), but DOM operations on the hidden banner stop. Eliminates
   wasted work after React mounts.

commits.subject: fix(debug): harden against circular refs, throwing toString, timer leaks
commits.body:
Three defensive fixes for edge cases that could crash or warn:

1. Circular references in entry details: JSON.stringify throws on
   circular objects, crashing the Log tab (separate root, no
   ErrorBoundary). Added safeStringify() in DebugPill and try/catch
   in debugGenerateReport — both fall back to '[unserializable]'.

2. Throwing toString in console interception: args.map(String) could
   throw if an argument has a Proxy or throwing toString(). Added
   safeString() wrapper — falls back to '[unstringifiable]'. Original
   console output is unaffected (called before debugAdd).

3. Timer leak in handleCopy: setTimeout for resetting copy label had
   no cleanup. Added copyTimerRef with clearTimeout on unmount and
   on re-click. Prevents React state-on-unmounted-component warning.

commits.subject: fix(debug): fix duplicate entries, HMR accumulation, clear state, source colors
commits.body:
Four bugs found via deep audit:

1. Duplicate entries on initial mount: setEntries(debugGetEntries()) +
   debugSubscribe replay doubled every entry. The subscription replays
   all current entries immediately, so the direct set was redundant.
   Removed it — subscription alone handles initial population.

2. Console interception HMR accumulation: console.error/warn patching
   had no HMR guard. Each Vite hot reload re-captured the already-patched
   methods as "originals," creating nested wrapper chains. After N reloads,
   each console.error produced N debug entries. Added __debugConsolePatched
   window flag matching the existing __debugLogListenersAttached pattern.

3. Clear button didn't reset reportText: if the visible textarea fallback
   was showing (clipboard failed), clicking Clear emptied entries but left
   the stale report text visible. Now resets reportText to null.

4. Missing import/export source colors: App.jsx uses debugAdd('import',
   ...) for file upload errors but SOURCE_COLORS had no entry, falling
   back to default gray. Added colors for both import and export sources.

Audit and fix 6 issues found in the initial debug system implementation:

1. Move embed-mode skip from DebugPill (conditional return before hooks,
   violates React rules) to main.jsx (don't mount component at all).
2. Remove unused status param from diagRow style function (dead code).
3. Add browser info and richer manifest validation (icon sizes, start_url,
   id) to PWA diagnostics tab per DEBUG_SYSTEM.md spec.
4. Add visible textarea fallback with auto-select when all clipboard
   methods fail, per spec's manual copy requirement.
5. Route Projects.jsx console.error through debugAdd for structured
   logging instead of relying on console interception.
6. Verify no duplicate entries from console interception + explicit
   debugAdd — all callers use one or the other, never both.

Fix 3 bugs, convert 3 vanilla DOM patterns to React, and close 2
accessibility gaps found during systematic HTML-to-React review.

Bugs:
- Fix body scroll lock race condition between overlays (new useScrollLock hook)
- Fix Chart.js theme colors going stale after dark/light toggle
- Fix SettingsPane label-select association for screen readers

React migration:
- Convert heatmap tooltip from vanilla DOM to React portal
- Move embed ?theme=/?bg= overrides from module scope into useEffect
- Consolidate URL param parsing into shared urlParams.js module

Accessibility:
- Add full keyboard navigation to FilterSidebar MultiSelect
- Add aria-labels to 12+ clickable elements across Health, Timeline, Progress

commits.subject: fix(build): exclude data dirs from Tailwind scanning, add test scenario
commits.body:
Tailwind v4 auto-scans all subdirectories of the CSS file. JSON data
files in public/data-commits/ and public/repos/ contain commit history
with Tailwind-like class names (e.g. "z-[9999] → z-[80]" from other
projects' changelogs). This caused phantom z-index CSS utilities in
the build output — unused but violating the "no ad-hoc values" rule.

Fix: @source not directives in styles.css exclude both directories.
Build output now contains only scale-mapped z-index values.

Also:
- Added z-index visual stacking test scenario (TESTING_GUIDE.md)
- Documented hamburger menu stacking context limitation (TODO.md):
  backdrop/dropdown trapped inside header z-21, drawers (z-30) render
  above the backdrop. Fix would require portaling — low priority.

commits.body:
Extends the initial debug pill fix (3ef0f9c) with a full codebase audit
per the glow-props Z_INDEX_SCALE pattern.

Fixes:
- .heatmap-tooltip: var(--z-toast) → var(--z-menu). The scale places
  tooltips in the menu/dropdown layer (50), not the toast layer (70).
  At z-70 the tooltip competed with toasts at the same level, and DOM
  order caused the tooltip to render above toasts — wrong.
- CSS scale comment now references Z_INDEX_SCALE.md (was BURGER_MENU.md)

Decision documentation added:
- Sub-layer rationale for --z-sticky-header (21), --z-drawer-backdrop
  (28), --z-modal-backdrop (58) — explains why they deviate from the
  base scale's "backdrop always 40" rule
- Inline debug pill comment explaining why zIndex:'80' is hardcoded
  (CSS variables unavailable before stylesheets load)

commits.subject: feat(pwa): add version.json polling for non-SW deployment detection
commits.body:
When a deployment only changes config (e.g. vercel.json headers) but no
code, the SW precache manifest stays identical and no update is detected.
This is how the X-Frame-Options stale cache issue persisted.

Added version.json polling (synctone/few-lap pattern):
- scripts/write-build-version.mjs writes { buildTime } at build time
- npm build runs it as a prebuild step
- pwa.js fetches on startup (3s delay) and hourly, compares with
  localStorage, surfaces mismatch as pwa-update-available
- applyUpdate() stores the new buildTime to prevent re-detection
- vercel.json serves version.json with no-cache headers
- Not precached by SW (not in globPatterns)
- Gitignored (generated file)

Tags: pwa, resilience, cross-project
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: pwa-hardening
Semver: minor

commits.subject: fix(pwa): add settle delay and surface waiting workers on visibility
commits.body:
Two gaps found comparing with synctone/few-lap patterns:

1. checkForUpdate() checked reg.waiting immediately after reg.update()
   resolved, but the update process is async — the waiting worker may
   not be populated yet. Added 1.5s settle delay (synctone/few-lap
   pattern).

2. visibilitychange handler called reg.update() but never surfaced the
   result. The onNeedRefresh callback from registerSW only fires once
   during setup — separate reg.update() calls don't re-trigger it.
   Now checks for waiting workers after settle delay and dispatches
   pwa-update-available manually.

commits.subject: fix(pwa): align SW update flow with synctone/few-lap/canva-grid patterns
commits.body:
Cross-project review found 4 issues:

1. skipWaiting/prompt conflict: skipWaiting:true in workbox config
   auto-activated new SWs, causing controllerchange reload before the
   user ever saw the update prompt. Removed — now user-controlled via
   applyUpdate() which sends SKIP_WAITING message.

2. Surprise reloads: controllerchange listener reloaded on ANY SW
   takeover, including background lifecycle events. Added
   _userClickedUpdate guard so only user-initiated updates reload.

3. False re-detection: After reload from update, onNeedRefresh could
   fire again for the same SW version. Added 30s sessionStorage
   suppression window (pattern from synctone/few-lap).

4. No recovery: If SW serves corrupt assets, app hangs on spinner
   forever. Added 30s recovery script that clears caches and
   unregisters SW (max 2 attempts per session, pattern from
   synctone/few-lap).

Tags: pwa, resilience, cross-project
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: pwa-hardening
Semver: minor

https://claude.ai/code/session_016hJj32ZoXvcDL5fCjPCDmD
commits.title: align SW update flow with synctone/few-lap/canva-grid patterns

commits.subject: fix(pwa): bypass SW navigation cache for embed URLs
commits.body:
The service worker precaches index.html with response headers from when
it was installed. If installed before the X-Frame-Options removal, the
cached response still included the old SAMEORIGIN header — blocking
cross-origin iframes. Since the iframe blocks before JS runs, the SW
update code never executes, creating a deadlock.

Added navigateFallbackDenylist for ?embed= URLs so they always bypass
the SW cache and go directly to the network (Vercel).

Tags: pwa, embed, bugfix

Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: embedding
Semver: patch

commits.subject: fix: add /embed path route for cross-origin iframe support
commits.body:
The X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN header (audit fix H4, 19e1e21)
blocked cross-origin iframes, breaking embeds in see-veo and other
apps. Vercel matches headers by path only, not query params, so
the /?embed= route couldn't be exempted.

Solution: Add /embed path with X-Frame-Options exempted in vercel.json.
New URL format: /embed?charts=id1,id2 (cross-origin safe).
Legacy /?embed= still works for same-origin use.

Changes:
- vercel.json: restore SAMEORIGIN, exempt /embed and /embed.js paths
- App.jsx: detect embed mode from /embed path + ?charts= param
- index.html: skip debug pill on /embed path
- EMBED_REFERENCE.md: document new /embed URL format

Note: see-veo needs to update EMBED_BASE or iframe URLs to use
/embed?charts= instead of /?embed= for cross-origin to work.

Tags: security, embed, bugfix
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: embed-system
Semver: patch

commits.subject: fix: remove X-Frame-Options header blocking cross-origin embeds
commits.body:
The SAMEORIGIN header added in H4 audit fix (19e1e21) blocked all
cross-origin iframes, breaking the embed feature. See-veo and other
apps embedding repo-tor charts via ?embed= got "refused to connect".
Vercel header matching is path-based only, so the embed.js exemption
didn't cover the HTML page loaded in iframes.

Removed the header entirely — the dashboard is public with no auth,
so there's no clickjacking risk. Cross-origin framing is intentional.

Tags: security, embed, bugfix

commits.subject: feat(menu): refactor HamburgerMenu to data-driven items pattern
commits.body:
Refactored HamburgerMenu to accept a data-driven `items` array prop
matching the glow-props BURGER_MENU.md reference pattern:

HamburgerMenu.jsx:
- Accepts `items` array: { label, action, icon?, visible?, separator?,
  external?, destructive?, highlight? }
- Filters by `visible` (default true) — never renders disabled items
- Renders optional: icons, separators between groups, external link
  indicators (arrow icon), destructive styling (red), highlight (primary)
- Labels truncated with text-overflow to prevent layout breaks
- focus-visible ring for keyboard users
- Arrow key navigation preserved (extra over reference pattern)
- Version footer preserved

Header.jsx:
- Builds menuItems array via useMemo with all standard menu items:
  - Quick Guide (always)
  - User Guide (always, external link to README)
  - Dark/Light mode toggle (always, separator before)
  - Save as PDF (always)
  - Install App (visible when installReady and not already installed)
  - Check for Updates (visible when updateAvailable, highlighted)
- Dark mode toggle dispatches SET_DARK_MODE to AppContext
- Icon SVGs extracted to module-level `icons` object

styles.css:
- Added .hamburger-item-icon, .hamburger-item-label (truncate),
  .hamburger-item-external, .hamburger-item-destructive

InstallInstructionsModal (InstallInstructionsModal.jsx):
- Data-driven modal renders whatever getInstallInstructions() returns
- Focus-trapped (useFocusTrap), Escape to close (useEscapeKey)
- Benefits section explains WHY to install for non-technical users

PWA install flow updated:

  format (was { title, steps }) for richer modal content

  topmost overlay closes when multiple are stacked (e.g., QuickGuide
  open over DetailPane).

  prevent subpixel rendering artifacts on non-Retina displays.

commits.subject: fix: critical and high-priority audit fixes (C1-C5, H1-H10)
commits.body:
Critical fixes:
- C1: Remove unconditional dark class in main.jsx that overrode flash
  prevention script, causing light mode users to see dark flash
- C2: Validate ?data= URL param — restrict to http/https schemes only,
  preventing SSRF via file://, data://, or internal network URLs
- C3: Replace postMessage wildcard '*' with document.referrer origin;
  add height bounds check and source validation in embed.js
- C4: Route ErrorBoundary errors to debug pill via __debugPushError
  so section-level crashes are visible without DevTools
- C5: Individual try/catch per month file fetch with user-facing warning
  listing which months failed to load (was silent data loss)

High-priority fixes:
- H1: Add viewport-fit=cover and safe-area-inset padding for notch/home
  indicator on iOS devices
- H2: Increase filter mode toggle button padding from 2px to 6px with
  min-height 32px (was far below 44px touch target)
- H4: Add security headers to vercel.json (X-Content-Type-Options,
  X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy) with CORS exception for embed.js
- H5: Route SW registration errors to debug pill
- H6: Wrap Header, TabBar, FilterSidebar, DetailPane, SettingsPane in
  ErrorBoundary so crashes don't blank the entire app
- H7: Replace TESTING_GUIDE dark mode toggle + private mode sections
  (features that don't exist) with actual theme test scenarios
- H8: Update CLAUDE.md dark mode status from "Partial" to "Implemented"

commits.subject: feat(theme): add light theme, flash prevention, cross-tab sync, system preference
commits.body:
Items 17-20 from cross-project alignment:

Light theme CSS variables (item 17):
- Restructure :root as light theme default, html.dark as override
- Full light palette: backgrounds (#F8F9FA), text (#1a1a1a), borders (#e5e7eb),
  shadows, chart grid, and reduced glow intensities
- Backward compatible: html starts with class="dark" for existing users

Flash prevention (item 18):
- Inline classic <script> in <head> reads darkMode from localStorage
- Applies .dark class before any stylesheet or module loads
- Falls back to prefers-color-scheme on first visit
- try/catch for sandboxed environments

Cross-tab sync (item 19):
- Storage event listener in AppContext syncs darkMode across tabs
- Only fires in OTHER tabs (no infinite loop)
- Dispatches SET_DARK_MODE action to update React state

System preference fallback (item 20):
- matchMedia listener tracks OS dark mode changes
- Only applies when user hasn't manually set a preference
- Once user toggles, their choice persists (not overridden by OS)

commits.subject: fix(ui): address review findings — accessibility, inline styles, constants
commits.body:
Fix all 6 findings from code review:

1. DetailPane: Move inline style to .detail-pane-empty CSS class
   (CLAUDE.md prohibits static inline styles)
2. HamburgerMenu: Add arrow key navigation between menu items
   (WAI-ARIA menu pattern expects up/down arrow support)
3. HamburgerMenu: Import version from package.json instead of
   hardcoding "v1.0.0" (stays in sync on version bumps)
4. QuickGuide: Focus modal container on open via useEffect +
   tabIndex={-1} (keyboard users need to know focus moved)
5. ShowMoreButton: Remove className prop, unify spacing to 12px
   margin-top in CSS (was inconsistent across grid vs list contexts)
6. Centralize pagination limits in PAGE_LIMITS constant in state.js
   (was scattered as magic numbers across 8 files)

Tags: accessibility, ui, refactor, clean

commits.subject: feat(ui): add responsive pagination across all list sections
commits.body:
Create useShowMore hook for consistent "Show more" pagination with
different limits for mobile vs desktop. Apply to 7 locations:

- DetailPane: 10 mobile / 20 desktop (was unbounded — could freeze)
- Timeline commits: 10 mobile / 25 desktop (was 100 — too many)
- Progress epics: 6 mobile / 12 desktop (was unbounded)
- Contributors: 6 mobile / 8 desktop (was hardcoded 8)
- Tags: 8 mobile / all desktop (was unbounded)
- Discover files: 5 mobile / 10 desktop (was hardcoded 10)
- Projects: 6 mobile / 12 desktop (was unbounded)

Each section shows a "Show more" button that loads the next batch.
Limits are calibrated to avoid overwhelming scroll on mobile while
considering co-located sections sharing the same tab.

Tags: ui, mobile, performance, ux
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: mobile-ux

Replace loose PDF/Install/Update buttons in the header with a
hamburger menu that groups secondary actions. Adds a 4-step Quick
Guide modal that auto-shows on first visit and is accessible anytime
from the menu.

Header now shows only 3 action elements: hamburger menu, filter
toggle, and settings gear — cleaner on all screen sizes.

Quick Guide steps:
1. Welcome — what the dashboard shows
2. Tabs — how to navigate between views
3. Filters — how to focus on specific data
4. View Level — how to change detail level

Tags: ui, ux, onboarding, mobile

Epic: mobile-ux

commits.subject: feat(dashboard): add PDF download button and fix PWA manifest
commits.body:
Two suggested implementations from glow-props now applied to code:

1. PWA manifest: Added `id: '/'` for stable app identity (prevents
   Chrome from breaking install on config changes) and
   `prefer_related_applications: false` (ensures beforeinstallprompt
   fires on Chromium).

2. PDF download: Added "PDF" button to Header that calls window.print().
   Enhanced @media print CSS with text color overrides, link underlines,
   break-inside:avoid on sections, and header styling. Added no-print
   wrappers to TabBar, FilterSidebar, DetailPane, SettingsPane so only
   dashboard content appears in the PDF.

Tags: pwa, ui, pdf
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: cross-project-alignment
Semver: minor

used across all devmade-ai projects. This ensures consistent AI assistant
behavior and documentation patterns.

- Add Suggested Implementations (PWA, Debug, Icons, PDF, Timer fix, HTTPS Proxy)
- Standardize Principles to glow-props 7 (extras moved to AI Notes)
- Replace Documentation subsection with standard 9-file managed set
- Move @coder/@data personas into AI Notes
- Rename AI_LESSONS.md → AI_MISTAKES.md (standard name)
- Rename USER_TESTING.md → TESTING_GUIDE.md (standard name)
- Update all active references across docs and source code

commits.subject: fix(dashboard): correct filter fallback, UTC date grouping, and nullish coalescing
commits.body:
Three bugs found during tab-by-tab data usage audit:

1. Filter fallback to unfiltered data — SummaryTab, TimelineTab, ProgressTab
   used `commitsLoaded && filteredCommits.length > 0` which fell through to
   unfiltered summary data when filters excluded all commits. Changed to
   `commitsLoaded` so empty filter results show zeros, not stale totals.

2. || vs ?? in utils.js and DiscoverTab — getAdditions(), getDeletions(),
   getFilesChanged() used || which treats 0 as falsy. DiscoverTab had 9
   instances of `c.stats?.additions || 0`. All changed to ?? operator.

3. Local time vs UTC date grouping — TimelineTab and ProgressTab used
   substring(0, 10/7) for date/month keys (local timezone from ISO string),
   but pre-aggregated keys use UTC. Added getUTCDateKey()/getUTCMonthKey()
   helpers and applied in both tabs.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01RbDmv8obRPnToLezqSGmHg
commits.title: correct filter fallback, UTC date grouping, and nullish coalescing

commits.subject: fix(pipeline): harden save-commit.js validation + use ?? in accumulateBucket
commits.body:
Pipeline audit found save-commit.js only checked field presence, not
values — allowing invalid data like impact:"infra" or complexity:99.
Added value validation matching merge-analysis.js: tags must be array,
complexity/urgency must be integer 1-5, impact must be canonical value.

Also changed || to ?? (nullish coalescing) in accumulateBucket for
stats.additions/deletions fallback so 0 is not treated as falsy.

Re-ran aggregation — no data changes (existing data already valid).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01RbDmv8obRPnToLezqSGmHg
commits.title: harden save-commit.js validation + use ?? in accumulateBucket

commits.subject: fix(data): use UTC consistently for all aggregation levels
commits.body:
Daily/monthly aggregation used substring(0,10) on timestamps (local
time) while weekly used new Date().getUTC*() (UTC). This caused 62
commits with non-zero timezone offsets to land in different daily/monthly
buckets vs weekly buckets. Fixed by adding getUTCDateKey/getUTCMonthKey
helpers used consistently across all aggregation levels and month file
grouping.

Also fixed 2 commits with impact:"infra" being silently dropped —
added alias mapping infra→infrastructure in calcImpactBreakdown,
accumulateBucket, contributor aggregation, and calcFilterOptions.

All 18 verification checks pass after re-aggregation.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01RbDmv8obRPnToLezqSGmHg
commits.title: use UTC consistently for all aggregation levels

commits.subject: feat(data): add time-windowed files + weekly/daily pre-aggregation
commits.body:
Redesign aggregation pipeline for time-windowed reporting:
- Summary file (data.json, ~126KB) with weekly/daily/monthly pre-aggregated
  buckets replaces 2.9MB monolith (96% smaller initial load)
- Per-month commit files (data-commits/YYYY-MM.json) for lazy loading
- Shared bucket helpers (createEmptyBucket/accumulateBucket/finalizeBucket)
  used by monthly, weekly, and daily aggregation functions
- Pre-computed filterOptions so FilterSidebar works without loading commits
- Two-phase dashboard loading: summary first, commits in background
- SummaryTab, TimelineTab, ProgressTab render from pre-aggregated data
  instantly; fall back to filteredCommits once loaded
- PWA runtime cache rule for data-commits/*.json files

commits.subject: fix: exclude incomplete months from trend charts
commits.body:
Monthly trend charts (Features vs Bug Fixes, Impact Over Time, Urgency,
Complexity, Debt) showed a misleading cliff for the current month when
only a few days of data existed (e.g., 39 commits from 2 days vs 800+
from full months).

Added excludeIncompleteLastMonth() utility that detects when the last
month's latest commit day is before the 15th and excludes it from trend
chart data. Applied to ProgressTab and useHealthData.

commits.subject: chore: migrate deployment from GitHub Pages to Vercel
commits.body:
GitHub Pages requires a 404.html hack for SPA routing, has no env var
injection at build, and needs manual "source" setting in repo UI.
Vercel handles all of this natively.

- Add vercel.json with SPA rewrite rule for client-side routing
- Delete .github/workflows/deploy.yml (GitHub Actions no longer needed)
- Change vite.config.js base from './' to '/' (Vercel serves at root)
- Update all live URL references to repo-tor.vercel.app
- Update ADMIN_GUIDE deployment section with Vercel setup instructions

commits.subject: fix: move debug pill to HTML level — shows even when JS bundle fails
commits.body:
The debug pill was created inside the JS bundle (main.jsx). If the bundle
failed to load, parse, or execute (stale SW cache, network error, JS crash),
the pill never appeared — leaving users with an infinite spinner and no way
to diagnose the problem.

Changes:
- index.html: inline <script> creates debug pill at HTML level with error
  capture (window.onerror, unhandledrejection), circular buffer (200 max),
  diagnostics view, and 10-second loading timeout warning
- main.jsx: removed ~170 lines of duplicate banner code; now bridges to
  HTML pill via window.__debugPushError() for React component stacks

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Qay1Ue4yRRGMQ39qDJHugS
commits.title: move debug pill to HTML level — shows even when JS bundle fails

commits.subject: feat: add embed.js auto-resize helper script
commits.body:
Embedders previously had to write their own postMessage listener to
get iframe auto-height working. Now they include one script tag:
  <script src=".../embed.js"></script>

The script auto-discovers all repo-tor iframes on the page and resizes
them when charts report their content height.

Docs updated to use script-tag as the primary approach, with the manual
listener moved to an "Advanced" section.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01G3sd64KAF9sQT7HnjsVrCe
commits.title: add embed.js auto-resize helper script

commits.subject: fix: use container height instead of document height for embed resize
commits.body:
The auto-height postMessage was measuring document.documentElement.scrollHeight
which included elements outside the embed container (#heatmap-tooltip div,
body pseudo-elements), reporting incorrect heights to the parent iframe.

Changes:
- Measure container.scrollHeight instead of document.documentElement.scrollHeight
- Delay initial height post by 100ms to let Chart.js finish first RAF render
- Track lastHeight to skip duplicate postMessage calls

https://claude.ai/code/session_01G3sd64KAF9sQT7HnjsVrCe
commits.title: use container height instead of document height for embed resize

commits.subject: fix: stop silently swallowing data loading errors causing black screen
commits.body:
The loading indicator flashed briefly then disappeared into a black screen
because .catch(() => {}) silently swallowed ALL fetch errors — not just the
expected 404 when no data file exists. Network failures, JSON parse errors,
and CORS issues were all hidden, leaving users with an invisible DropZone
on the dark background.

Changes:
- Only treat HTTP 404 as expected (no data file); show error card + retry
  button for all other failures
- Add dashboard-enter fade-in animation (0.3s) for smooth loading transition
- Fix PWA dynamic import to properly catch promise rejection
- Make DropZone more visible with title heading and vertical centering

commits.subject: feat: add role-based view levels (Executive/Management/Developer)
commits.body:
Add view level selector that changes data granularity for different audiences
while keeping the same dashboard layout:

- Executive: aggregated totals, weekly heatmap, summary-only drilldowns
- Management: per-repo groupings, daily heatmap, summary drilldowns
- Developer: individual contributors, hourly heatmap, full commit lists (default)

Implementation includes:
- VIEW_LEVELS config with granularity settings per level

commits.subject: feat: add .env file support for GH_TOKEN authentication
commits.body:
Enable API extraction to work in AI sessions and non-interactive
environments by supporting GH_TOKEN via .env file.

Changes:
- Add .env.example template with GH_TOKEN configuration
- Update extract-api.js to load .env and use GH_TOKEN
- Update setup-gh.sh with --save-env flag and .env support
- Add .env to .gitignore (secrets shouldn't be committed)

Authentication priority:
1. GH_TOKEN environment variable
2. .env file in project root
3. gh auth login (interactive)

This enables AI assistants to use API extraction by simply creating
a .env file with their GitHub token.
commits.title: add .env file support for GH_TOKEN authentication

and tested from any environment.

commits.subject: feat: expand tag colors with presets and dynamic fallback
commits.body:
- Add 30+ preset tag colors for common conventional commit types:
  - Green family: feature, enhancement, seed, init
  - Red family: bugfix, fix, security, hotfix
  - Orange/amber: removal, revert, deprecate
  - Purple family: refactor, naming, cleanup
  - Yellow: test, test-unit, test-e2e
  - Orange: build, ci, deploy
  - Slate: config, chore
  - Pink family: style, ux, ui, accessibility
  - Cyan: performance, perf
  - Lime: dependency, deps

- Add hash-based dynamic color generation for unknown tags
  - Uses 10-color palette optimized for dark backgrounds
  - Same tag always gets same color (consistent hashing)

commits.subject: fix: capture validation failures for reprocessing instead of skipping
commits.body:
Both scripts now write malformed commits to needs-reprocess.json files
instead of silently skipping them:

save-commit.js:
- Validates all required fields before saving
- Valid commits are saved, invalid ones written to needs-reprocess.json
- Exits with error if any commits failed validation
- Clear error messages explaining what went wrong

aggregate-processed.js:
- Loudly reports all malformed commits during aggregation
- Writes them to processed/<repo>/needs-reprocess.json
- Shows warning at end of output

This ensures validation failures are visible and actionable rather
than silently lost.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01GTJnfM2q8ybazZtZ1Y54fg

commits.subject: fix: add validation to save-commit.js to prevent incomplete saves
commits.body:
Root cause of malformed commits: when AI analyzed batches, it only
output analysis fields instead of complete commit objects. Without
validation, these incomplete commits were saved to processed/.

Now save-commit.js validates:
- Required metadata: sha, timestamp, subject
- Required author: author_id or author
- Required analysis: tags, complexity, urgency, impact

Fails fast with clear error message if any fields are missing.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01GTJnfM2q8ybazZtZ1Y54fg
commits.title: add validation to save-commit.js to prevent incomplete saves

The renderSecurity() function was trying to update a non-existent element 'security-count', causing a TypeError that stopped execution of applyFilters(). Since renderSummary() is called after renderSecurity() in applyFilters(), the Overview tab never updated when filters changed.

The security count is already correctly handled by renderHealth() via the 'health-security-count' element, so the broken line was simply removed.

- Resolves raw emails to canonical author IDs
- Normalizes author_id in all commits

commits.subject: fix: normalize data field formats in dashboard
commits.body:
Add helper functions to handle inconsistent field formats:
- getCommitSubject(): handles subject vs message
- getAdditions(): handles stats.additions vs lines_added
- getDeletions(): handles stats.deletions vs lines_deleted

This ensures all 576 commits display correctly regardless of which extraction format they came from.

commits.subject: fix: dashboard Files Changed showing 0
commits.body:
Add getFilesChanged() helper to handle multiple data formats:
- stats.filesChanged (new extractions)
- files_changed (snake_case, older commits)

Now correctly shows 1,689 files changed across 576 commits.

commits.subject: refactor: migrate from batch files to individual commit files
commits.body:
Store each analyzed commit as a separate JSON file instead of batching 15 commits per file. This simplifies deduplication (file existence = processed), enables atomic edits, and produces cleaner git diffs.

Changes:
- Add scripts/save-commit.js for saving individual commits
- Add scripts/migrate-batches-to-commits.js for one-time migration
- Update scripts/aggregate-processed.js to read from commits/
- Update scripts/extract.js to write individual commit files
- Migrate all 4 repos (576 commits total)
- Delete old batches/ directories

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.subject: feat: add aggregation script for processed data with urgency/impact
commits.body:
New script reads AI-analyzed commits from processed/<repo>/batches/
and generates dashboard-ready JSON with full aggregations:

- tagBreakdown, complexityBreakdown (existing)
- urgencyBreakdown, impactBreakdown (NEW)
- Monthly aggregations with avgUrgency, impact distribution
- Contributor aggregations with avgUrgency, impactBreakdown

Output structure:
- dashboard/data.json (overall, all repos combined)
- dashboard/repos/<repo>.json (per-repo, same schema)

Both views use identical schema enabling consistent dashboard code.
commits.title: add aggregation script for processed data with urgency/impact

commits.subject: docs: complete Dashboard V2 design specification
commits.body:
New hybrid dashboard architecture combining logical groupings with
contextual detail pane. Reduces cognitive load (4 tabs instead of 7)
while enabling in-context drill-down.

Key design decisions:
- Overview/Activity/Work/Health tab structure
- Detail pane slides out on click (preserves context)
- Same data schema for overall and per-repo views
- Urgency and Impact dimensions integrated throughout

Files:
- DASHBOARD_V2_DESIGN.md: Full design spec with layouts and schemas
- SESSION_NOTES.md: Updated current state and next actions
- TODO.md: Reorganized for V2 implementation priorities
- HISTORY.md: Added design session entry
commits.title: complete Dashboard V2 design specification

commits.subject: feat: add incremental processing support for "feed the chicken"
commits.body:
Add manifest-based tracking to enable resumable batch processing:
- scripts/pending.js: Generates pending batches by comparing manifests
  against extracted commits. Supports --rebuild-manifests flag.
- scripts/manifest-update.js: Updates manifest after batch approval
- processed/<repo>/manifest.json: Tracks processed SHAs (source of truth)
- pending/<repo>/batches/: Generated batches of unprocessed commits only

This allows "feed the chicken" to work correctly even after merging
and starting a new session - only truly new/unprocessed commits will
be presented for review.

Updated EXTRACTION_PLAYBOOK.md with new workflow documentation.
commits.title: add incremental processing support for "feed the chicken"

commits.subject: feat: add urgency and impact dimensions to commit analysis schema
commits.body:
Two new dimensions for richer commit insights:

Urgency (1-5): How critical was the change?
- Captures reactive vs planned work patterns
- Helps identify operational health (high urgency = problems)

Impact (internal/user-facing/infrastructure/api): Who is affected?
- Shows where effort goes
- Distinguishes building vs maintaining vs supporting

commits.subject: docs: comprehensive 55+ tag list for commit analysis
commits.body:
Expanded from 11 generic tags to 55+ specific tags across categories:
- User-facing: feature, enhancement, bugfix, hotfix, ui, ux, accessibility, i18n, localization
- Code: refactor, simplify, removal, deprecation, migration, naming, types
- Performance: performance, memory, caching
- Security: security, auth, authorization, vulnerability, sanitization
- Testing: test-unit, test-integration, test-e2e, test-fix, coverage, mocks
- Docs: docs, changelog, comments, api-docs, examples
- Infra: ci, cd, docker, monitoring, hosting
- Build: build, bundler, config, env, lint, formatter
- Deps: dependency-add, dependency-update, dependency-remove, dependency-security
- Database: database, schema, data-migration, seed
- API: api, api-breaking, endpoint
- Git: merge, revert, release, init
- Style: style, imports, whitespace
- Errors: error-handling, logging, validation

AI reads full commit body and assigns ALL applicable tags.

commits.subject: docs: add research items for commit model improvements
commits.body:
New Research/Investigation section in TODO.md:

1. Device/Platform Attribution
   - Split contributions by committer name (mobile vs desktop)
   - Config mapping for name variations with device tags

2. AI-Powered Commit Categorization
   - Claude reads commit messages + diffs to understand intent
   - Auto-tagging beyond conventional commit types
   - Confidence scores and human override

3. Multi-Tag Commit Model
   - One commit can have multiple tags (feat + refactor + docs)
   - Schema change from single type to tags[] with weights
   - Backward compatibility with existing data

4. Tag-Centric Reporting
   - Shift focus from commit counts to accomplishments
   - Effort weighting based on complexity
   - Activity vs output distinction

commits.subject: docs: add commit convention guide and validation hooks
commits.body:
D2 deliverable - Commit Convention Guide:
- docs/COMMIT_CONVENTION.md: Full guide for conventional commits
  with type definitions, examples, and analytics impact
- .gitmessage: Git commit template for consistent formatting
- hooks/commit-msg: Validation hook for commit message format
- hooks/setup.sh: Installation script for git hooks

Run ./hooks/setup.sh to enable validation.

commits.subject: Add Git Analytics Reporting System
commits.body:
- scripts/extract.js: Node.js extraction script with git log parsing,
  conventional commit detection, keyword-based type fallback, and
  structured JSON output
- scripts/extract.sh: Shell wrapper for easier usage
- dashboard/index.html: Static analytics dashboard with Chart.js
  - Timeline: daily commit chart, recent commits list
  - Progress: monthly volume, cumulative growth, feature vs fix trends
  - Contributors: commits and lines by author
  - Security: highlighted security-related commits
  - Types: pie chart and percentage breakdown
- Updated .gitignore to exclude generated reports/
- Updated docs with usage documentation
